coreTruthSet.description: The Yatasi are framed as a close variation of the Natchitoches baseline rather than a completely separate system.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: The Yatasi are not a completely different world. They are a close variation of the Natchitoches baseline.

coreTruthSet.description: Yatasi trade behavior is framed as participatory and dependency-oriented rather than central node control.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: They are not driving trade. They are participating within it.

coreTruthSet.description: Diplomacy is portrayed as personal-trust-first and relationship-driven rather than large-scale strategic positioning.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: More intimate, relationship-driven diplomacy... less strategic positioning in large-scale alliances.

coreTruthSet.description: The profile emphasizes weakened or fragmented settlement continuity, with greater vulnerability to external pressure.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Historically weakened and reduced over time... more fragmented settlement identity.

coreTruthSet.description: Language variation is framed as subtle and insider-legible (tone/expression/cadence) despite close dialect proximity.
coreTruthSet.quoteExcerpt: Likely very close dialectically... subtle differences in tone, expression, cadence.

settingAnchors.name: Yatasi Interior Tributary Zone
settingAnchors.description: Interior-facing habitation and movement space less exposed to main-artery traffic and visibility pressures.

continuityConstraints.title: Personal Trust Before Negotiation
continuityConstraints.text: Formal negotiation follows personal trust formation; transactional entry without trust weakens long-horizon alignment.

continuityConstraints.title: Relationship Entry Over Traffic Entry
continuityConstraints.summary: Access protocol constraint for Yatasi-facing scenes.
continuityConstraints.text: Influence with the Yatasi begins through trusted relationships rather than route control or traffic dominance.

continuityConstraints.title: Stabilize Access, Not Trade Domination
continuityConstraints.text: Legitimate leverage comes from stabilizing Yatasi access and reciprocity, not from imposing intermediary control.

openResearchGaps.title: What dated milestones best map Yatasi settlement weakening and merger dynamics with neighboring groups?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to avoid compressing multi-decade structural changes into a single narrative beat.

openResearchGaps.title: What linguistic markers (tone, cadence, phrase choices) can be safely treated as Yatasi-distinct in dialogue?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed for voice differentiation that avoids generic or over-assertive language claims.

openResearchGaps.title: Which concrete exchange dependencies defined Yatasi trade participation under Natchitoches-adjacent systems?
openResearchGaps.description: Needed to model stabilization scenes with credible economic specifics.
